analysis_result: cg26878949 (TSS1500, Island, beta 0.520 vs 0.190) and cg13227982 (TSS1500, S_Shore, 0.889 vs 0.628). Only 1/2 matches. cg01512589 not returned by model's top-2 selection based on delta-beta+t-test.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. COMPARISON
   - Reported (paper, main text p. 5-6, Fig. 3f legend): 'CpGs in the promoter region (cg01512589 and cg26878949) of PTCH1 ... to be hypermethylated in MPNST-G1 but not in MPNST-G2.' The paper explicitly names both probes and uses both together in the Fig. 3f correlation (r = -0.5129, P = 0.0002).
   - Model: cg26878949 (TSS1500, Island, beta 0.520 vs 0.190) and cg13227982 (TSS1500, S_Shore, 0.889 vs 0.628). 1/2 match.

B. CONCORDANCE
   - Exact set-match: 1 of 2 probes agree (cg26878949). cg01512589 is missing from the model output; cg13227982 was returned instead.
   - Applying the >=10% relative agreement rule to probe identity: this is a categorical/identity comparison, not a numeric proportion. Agreement rate 50% is below the 90% concordance threshold. Classified DISCREPANT.

C. INVESTIGATION
   - All three probes (cg01512589, cg26878949, cg13227982) are present in GSE207207_average_beta_values.txt.gz, so data availability is not the issue.
   - Spot-check of beta values from the beta matrix for four samples (M2372, M3048, M2346, M2252):
       cg01512589: 0.151, 0.403, 0.134, 0.475 (modest, intermediate)
       cg26878949: 0.434, 0.833, 0.416, 0.763 (strong G1 vs G2 contrast plausible)
       cg13227982: 0.834, 0.963, 0.877, 0.962 (very high in all; small G1 vs G2 delta even if the model reports 0.889 vs 0.628)
   - cg01512589 shows a smaller raw beta spread and lower absolute values than cg26878949. A top-2 ranking that emphasizes delta-beta magnitude alone will preferentially pick probes with larger absolute beta differences such as cg13227982 (which the model scored 0.889 vs 0.628 -> delta 0.26) over cg01512589 (likely delta ~0.15-0.20).
   - The paper did not report using a delta-beta + t-test ranking to select these two CpGs. The paper's selection appears to be biologically/anatomically motivated: it picked probes in the PTCH1 promoter region that co-track with expression in the correlation analysis shown in Fig. 3f. Both cg01512589 and cg26878949 are in the TSS1500/Island region of PTCH1. cg13227982 is in the S_Shore (shore of the island), a slightly different genomic context, and would plausibly be excluded if the authors restricted attention to TSS1500 + Island annotations only.

D. ROOT CAUSE
   - Variable-choice / selection-criterion mismatch. The model's algorithm (top-2 by delta-beta + t-test over PTCH1 promoter probes) is a reasonable but not paper-exact approach. The authors likely restricted to TSS1500 + Island (excluding shores) and/or required correlation with gene expression, which would promote cg01512589 into the selected pair and exclude cg13227982.

E. MAGNITUDE
   - 1 of 2 probes correct (50% set agreement). One named probe (cg26878949) reproduced exactly; the other reported probe (cg01512589) substituted with a nearby shore probe (cg13227982). Biological conclusion (PTCH1 promoter hypermethylation in MPNST-G1) is preserved, so this is a probe-identity mismatch, not a conclusion mismatch. MAJOR in terms of literal probe ID concordance (>20% of the reported probes wrong); MINOR in terms of scientific substance.

F. PROPOSED FIX
   - Restrict the candidate probe set to PTCH1 promoter probes annotated as Relation_to_Island == 'Island' (drop N_Shore, S_Shore, N_Shelf, S_Shelf, OpenSea). That would eliminate cg13227982 (S_Shore) and leave cg01512589 and cg26878949 as the top-2 differentially methylated probes between MPNST-G1 and MPNST-G2.
   - Alternative: rank probes by correlation of beta with PTCH1 expression (negative correlation, as used in Fig. 3f) rather than by delta-beta + t-test alone. The paper explicitly anchors these probes by gene-expression correlation (r = -0.5129, P = 0.0002).

G. CONFIDENCE
   - HIGH that the cause is the probe-selection/annotation filter (Island vs Shore) and that applying an Island-only filter would recover the paper's pair.
proposed_fix: Restrict the candidate probe set to PTCH1 promoter CpGs with Relation_to_Island == 'Island' (drop shores/shelves/OpenSea). This removes cg13227982 (S_Shore) from contention and leaves cg01512589 and cg26878949, matching the paper. Alternatively, rank candidates by correlation of beta with PTCH1 mRNA expression (Pearson r, negative direction) as the paper does in Fig. 3f, rather than by delta-beta + t-test alone.
